exercises.title: Shell Arithmetic
exercises.description: In the Python shell, expressions are evaluated immediately. Print the number of hours in a year by calculating 365 * 24.
exercises.starterCode:
# TODO: Print the number of hours in a year
# Hint: hours_in_year = days_per_year * hours_per_day
hints: Use print() to display the result of 365 * 24.
The * operator is multiplication in Python.

exercises.title: String Repetition Discovery
exercises.description: One of Python's interesting features is that you can multiply a string by a number. Print 'Ha' repeated 5 times, then print 'Python! ' repeated 3 times on the next line.
exercises.starterCode:
# TODO: Print 'Ha' multiplied by 5
# TODO: Print 'Python! ' multiplied by 3
hints: String * number repeats the string that many times.

exercises.title: type() Function
exercises.description: Python has a built-in type() function that tells you what kind of data something is. Print the type of 42, then the type of 'hello', then the type of 3.14.
exercises.starterCode:
# TODO: Print the type of 42
# TODO: Print the type of 'hello'
# TODO: Print the type of 3.14
hints: Use print(type(42)) to print the type of 42.
int = integer (whole number), str = string (text), float = decimal number.

warmup.title: Quick Recall: print() Basics
warmup.description: You need print() to display output in a .py file. What does this code output?
warmup.starterCode:
# Predict, then run
print("Hello from VS Code!")
